child_metadata.hypothesis: For the pure alternating trace, either a seven-block schedule-head cycle or dyadic endpoint parity supplies a closed induction state; if not, excess degree may give an exact finite-support target.
child_metadata.interpretation: The proposed seven-block schedule-head cycle is exactly refuted at block 153. The dyadic endpoint parity candidate is exactly refuted at exponent 11. The exact depth-two root-activity portrait fails to preserve even the zero status of the next emitted block. The exact fringe transition graph is strongly connected, so no nonconstant monotone observable depending only on its two-bit state can hold for all local tails. Any surviving period-two argument must retain nonlocal tail information or use a different arithmetic invariant. The exact excess-degree identity reduces the surviving target to proving that the block index minus the leading-t run of H_m is unbounded; the bounded checks do not prove that asymptotic claim.
